findings.summary: infrastructure/core/cli.py imports directly from infrastructure/project at runtime, creating a downward dependency from core into a peer package
evidence: infrastructure/core/cli.py line 33: `from infrastructure.project.discovery import discover_projects` — unconditional runtime import
infrastructure/core/multi_project.py correctly guards the same import with TYPE_CHECKING, showing the pattern is known but not consistently applied
coupling matrix shows infrastructure/core/ imports from infrastructure/project/ (2 edges)
findings.suggestion: Move the discover_projects call out of core/cli.py into a higher-level coordinator (e.g., the scripts/ layer or a dedicated multi-project module), keeping core/ free of project/-layer dependencies. Alternatively, apply the TYPE_CHECKING guard already used in multi_project.py if only type annotations are needed.

findings.summary: core/ mixes three error strategies — raise (103 sites), return None (28 sites), and result_type (4 sites) — without a clear boundary rule separating them
evidence: holistic_context errors.strategy_by_directory core/: try_catch=147, throws=103, returns_null=28, result_type=4
infrastructure/core/exceptions.py documents a clear exceptions.py vs errors.py boundary rule, but no equivalent rule separates raise-vs-return-None within module logic
infrastructure/validation/ shows a similar mix: try_catch=66, throws=17, returns_null=15, result_type=2 — the pattern is codebase-wide, not isolated to one module
findings.suggestion: Establish and document an explicit rule: I/O boundary functions that callers are expected to handle gracefully may return None/empty; internal logic functions should raise typed exceptions from exceptions.py. Apply the rule consistently within each module, using the existing chain_exceptions() helper to preserve context on rethrow.

findings.summary: infrastructure/validation/audit_orchestrator.py has multiple bare except Exception catches that log but do not re-raise or return a structured failure signal
evidence: Lines 77, 121, 175, 197: `except Exception as e:` — each logs a warning and continues, silently degrading audit completeness without surfacing partial-failure to the caller
The run_comprehensive_audit() return type is ScanResults with no failure flag, so callers cannot distinguish a complete scan from one where multiple phases errored out
findings.suggestion: Accumulate per-phase errors into ScanResults (add an errors list field or reuse existing issue structures), so callers can detect degraded audit runs without guessing from absence of results.

findings.summary: scripts/02_run_analysis.py and scripts/06_llm_review.py are outliers among 18 sibling scripts: they do not call get_logger/log_header/log_success at the module level, while 16/18 siblings do
evidence: sibling_behavior scripts/: get_logger count=16/18, log_header count=16/18, log_success count=14/18
scripts/02_run_analysis.py confirmed missing log_header at module level (imports it inside main() only via local import)
scripts/06_llm_review.py listed as missing log_success in outlier list
findings.suggestion: Move the log_header call and get_logger instantiation in scripts/02_run_analysis.py to module scope consistent with sibling scripts. Add log_success calls in scripts/06_llm_review.py where stage completion is reported.

findings.summary: infrastructure/llm/core/client.py has 1 wide-param function and 187 config-bag references, making LLMConfig an implicit catch-all parameter bag passed through multiple call layers
evidence: LLMConfig dataclass carries temperature, max_tokens, top_p, top_k, seed, stop, format_json, repeat_penalty, num_ctx — GenerationOptions duplicates several of these for per-query overrides, creating two parallel config shapes passed through the same call chain
findings.suggestion: Separate connection config (base_url, model, timeout) from generation defaults (temperature, seed, max_tokens) at the LLMConfig level, and eliminate the GenerationOptions/LLMConfig duplication by having GenerationOptions be the sole per-call override applied on top of a lean connection config. This reduces the parameter bag surface without adding indirection.
